reasoning: The market asks if between 10 and 12 (inclusive) Starship launches will successfully reach space during the calendar year 2025. As of the knowledge cutoff (November 9, 2025), SpaceX has conducted exactly 5 Starship launches in 2025 (Starship Flights 7 through 11). "Flight 11", which launched on October 13, 2025, was the 11th Starship flight overall and the 5th test flight of 2025. Recent news and space community discussions from early November indicate that SpaceX has announced Starship Flight 12 will not happen until 2026. Because only 5 flights have occurred in 2025, the total number of launches is far below the 10-12 requirement, making a YES resolution impossible by the end of the year. Due to system bounds, I am setting the probability to the minimum allowed 0.05.
